programs.title: Restored
programs.desc: The owner of the first house Brett tackled has purchased a Victorian home and needs help again; the project includes bringing back original exterior elements and colors as well as a complete restoration of the living room, mudroom and kitchen.

programs.title: Restored
programs.desc: Brett helps a woman and her adult children restore their majestic 1913 Mission Revival mansion; he's elated when he realizes he can restore the original linoleum in the kitchen, but the homeowners may not be open to the idea.

programs.title: Restored
programs.desc: Brett is called upon to restore a historically significant 1906 Arts and Crafts home; he brings it back to its original glory by reintroducing the closed-in fireplaces, reinstating the floor plan and replacing the 1980s kitchen.

programs.title: Restored
programs.desc: In Redlands, Calif., Brett restores a 1941 modern ranch for a couple who inherited the home; he modernizes the kitchen and opens up the living and dining room, then he surprises the couple by adding a feature found in the original blueprints.

programs.title: Restored
programs.desc: Brett's clients purchase their first home together, a 1923 Mediterranean Revival, and they want to restore it after years of small updates by the former homeowners; during demolition, Brett discovers the house is suffering from severe termite damage.

programs.title: Happy to Be Home
programs.sub_title: Happy to Help an Old Friend
programs.desc: Gray helps Grumpy's lifelong friend renovate a historic 1910 cottage; Gray worries that this friend, who brings as much grump to the project as Grumpy, will struggle to accept the color and whimsy she brings.
